title: Law as a Vocation for Justice: Reflections on Promoting the Common Good in Mindanao
content: In reading Louis Wehle’s article, I came to understand that law is not meant to be a cold or purely technical system, but rather a human instrument directed toward the common good. At the outset, Wehle emphasizes that law must respond to the concrete realities of people’s lives, especially those who are poor and marginalized. In this light, I see how the law becomes especially meaningful in Mindanao, where many communities continue to experience poverty, conflict, and historical injustice. For instance, Indigenous Peoples, Bangsamoro communities, and other vulnerable groups rely on the law to affirm their human dignity and to correct long-standing inequalities through the protection of rights, land, and identity.Moreover, Wehle makes it clear that the legal profession is not simply a career but a social vocation. From this perspective, I understand that lawyers are called to serve justice before self-interest, a responsibility that is particularly important in Mindanao. In practice, this calling is fulfilled when lawyers provide legal aid, engage in pro bono work, and educate communities about their rights and responsibilities. As a result, justice becomes more accessible to those who would otherwise be excluded from the legal system. Consistent with Wehle’s view, I believe that lawyers who stand with the poor and powerless help transform the law into an instrument of empowerment rather than oppression. In addition, Wehle emphasized the role of law in peace-building and social harmony. According to him, justice is the foundation of lasting peace, and without it, conflict inevitably persists. Within the context of Mindanao, where violence and division have long shaped its history, the fair and humane application of the law provides peaceful mechanisms for resolving disputes and promoting reconciliation. Consequently, trust in legal institutions is strengthened. Thus, I see how law and the legal profession can genuinely promote social justice and the common good for the people of Mindanao.
